content.problemStatement: Rectify the errors in the program to correctly print the last character of the string.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a non‑empty string S (printable ASCII characters, no leading or trailing spaces).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single character – the last character of the given string S – on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The only test case string is "CodeChef"; its last character is 'f'.
First string "hello" ends with 'o'. Second string "A" ends with 'A'.
Each line's last character is printed: 'z' from "xyz", '5' from "12345", and '#' from "!@#".

solution.approach: For each test case we read the whole line representing the string. The last character is at index length‑1 (or using language‑specific helpers like back() or charAt()). We output that character. This is O(|S|) per test case for reading, and O(1) extra work to fetch the last character.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total number of characters in the input) – each character is read once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) besides the input buffer used by the language runtime.
